title: The Fastest Way to Set Up Digital Product Payment Solutions in West Africa and Start Selling
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: You've been selling your digital products for months, but PayPal and Stripe keep blocking you due to suspicious activity or you're just not in their allowed countries. Ghana's thriving digital creator community is no exception, and it's time for a solution that works - regardless of your bank's restrictions or the payment processor's limitations. The good news is that you can still sell your ebooks, courses, and software, but now you'll need to bypass the conventional payment systems and use a more reliable and secure alternative. It's time to break free from the limitations and get back to what you do best: creating.
What You Can Actually Get Done Today

To start selling your digital products today, you need to focus on what really matters - setting up a payment solution that works, no matter what. You can accomplish this by installing a wallet connector like Web3 Wallet Connect, which costs just $19.99 and integrates seamlessly with your existing website. This will enable you to accept payments from your customers directly, without relying on PayPal or Stripe. The Minimum Viable Setup That Works

A viable digital product payment setup is not a complex system, but it does require some basic components. You'll need a payment processor that works with your country's banking system, a secure way to handle transactions, and a system to deliver your digital products to your customers. With Web3 Wallet Connect, you get all these components in one place, including automated delivery and payment verification. By keeping your setup simple, you'll be less likely to run into issues down the line, and more likely to make sales.

Don't get bogged down in unnecessary details like setting up a complex e-commerce platform or integrating multiple payment gateways. Focus on the bare minimum that you need to get started, and you'll be amazed at how quickly you can set up a working payment solution. Remember, you can always add more features later, but for now, just focus on getting paid.
Don't worry about the technical details; just focus on setting up a payment solution that works.
